back_translation: In the beginning, God created the heavens and the earth.
The earth was still without form and empty; darkness was over the surface of the deep waters, and the Spirit of God was moving over the surface of the waters.
God said, "Let there be light," and the light came into being.
God saw that the light was good, and God separated the light from the darkness.
God called the light "day," and called the darkness "night." There was evening and there was morning—the first day.
God said, "Let there be an expanse between the waters, to separate water from water."
So God made the expanse and separated the water that was below the expanse from the water that was above the expanse. And it was so.
God called the expanse "sky." There was evening and there was morning—the second day.
God said, "Let the waters under the sky be gathered together into one place, and let the dry land appear." And it was so.
God called the dry land "earth," and called the place where the waters had gathered "seas." And God saw that this was good.
God said, "Let the earth bring forth vegetation: plants bearing seed, and trees that bear fruit according to their kinds upon the earth, with their seed inside their fruit." And it was so.
The earth then brought forth vegetation: plants bearing seed according to their kinds, and trees bearing fruit with their seed inside according to their kinds. And God saw that this was good.
There was evening and there was morning—the third day.
God said, "Let there be lights in the expanse to separate the day from the night, and let them serve as signs to mark seasons, days, and years.
And let them be lights in the expanse to give light upon the earth." And it was so.
So God made two great lights: the greater light to govern the day, and the lesser light to govern the night, and He also made the stars.
God set those lights in the expanse to give light upon the earth,
to govern both daytime and nighttime, and to separate the light from the darkness. And God saw that this was good.
There was evening and there was morning—the fourth day.
God said, "Let the waters be filled with swarms of living creatures, and let birds fly above the earth in the expanse across the sky."

So God created the great sea creatures and every kind of living creature that moves in the waters according to their kinds, and every kind of winged bird according to its kind. And God saw that this was good.
God blessed them, saying, "Be fruitful and multiply and fill the waters in the seas, and let the birds multiply on the earth."
There was evening and there was morning—the fifth day.
God said, "Let the earth bring forth living creatures according to their kinds: livestock, crawling things, and wild animals according to their kinds." And it was so.
God made the wild animals according to their kinds, the livestock according to their kinds, and every kind of crawling creature on the earth according to its kind. And God saw that this was good.

God said, "Let us make humanity in our image, after our likeness; and let them rule over the fish in the sea, the birds in the sky, the livestock, all the earth, and every kind of crawling creature that moves upon the earth."
So God created humanity in His image; in the image of God He created him; male and female He created them.

God blessed them and said to them, "Be fruitful and multiply, fill the earth and subdue it, and rule over the fish in the sea, the birds in the sky, and every kind of living creature that moves upon the earth."
God said, "Behold, I have given you every kind of seed-bearing plant on all the earth and every tree whose fruit contains seed within it, to be your food.
And I have given every kind of green plant as food to all the wild animals on the earth, every bird in the sky, and every kind of creature moving on the earth that has breath in it." And it was so.
God saw everything that He had made, and behold, all of it was very good. There was evening and there was morning—the sixth day.
